visual_notes: Amethyst geodes from Brazil and Uruguay are among the most recognizable mineral display specimens globally. Hollow cavite filled with violet crystals pointing inward. The finest deep Siberian-grade material shows a red flash under incandescent light. Color zoning in angular patterns is characteristic and diagnostic.
prompt_fragments: amethyst geode cross-section — violet crystal cathedral in basalt egg
deep Siberian amethyst with red flash under incandescent light
Dionysus purple: iron-radiation alchemy crystallized in quartz
vibe_notes: Amethyst is the purple of wine, prayer, and power — used by bishops, kings, and Dionysus worshippers alike. A geode of amethyst is a cathedral floor inside a stone egg: thousands of violet crystals pointing inward at nothing and everything.
folklore_summary: The name 'amethyst' comes from Greek 'amethystos' (not intoxicated). Greek myth: Dionysus, god of wine, pursued a maiden named Amethystos who prayed to the goddess Artemis for protection; Artemis turned her to white quartz. Dionysus, repentant, poured wine over the crystal — turning it purple. Ancie
